Fort Myers_FL.title: 🏗️ A crane is now up in downtown Fort Myers for the first high ...
Fort Myers_FL.caption:
🏗️ A crane is now up in downtown Fort Myers for the first high rise to be built in 20 years. Developer JAXI, along with SERHANT, recently held a “going vertical” celebration across the street from the construction site. Mayor Kevin Anderson called it a major milestone for downtown Fort Myers. The project is expected to be completed next year.

🏨 Another crane is also up in Naples at the site of the new JW Marriott hotel on the corner of US 41 and Palm Street. The 225,000 square foot hotel will bring 331 rooms to the area and is expected to be a major addition to the Naples hospitality market.

🚗 Right in between Naples and Fort Myers, something unique is being built. The Estero MotorPlex, described as the ultimate man cave for your vehicle or motor coach, is under construction. Ronnie DeChambeau with SERHANT is representing the project. Plans call for 40 luxury storage units on five acres, ranging from about 1,300 to more than 2,700 square feet, with prices from the mid 600s to around 1.3 million dollars.

🎭 And finally, the historic Florida Repertory Theatre in downtown Fort Myers celebrated opening night of Little Shop of Horrors. It is the only professional equity theater in Lee County, and truly the closest thing to Broadway in Southwest Florida. Longtime theater supporter Stephanie Davis says the acting is as great as Broadway, sometimes even better. I was honored to attend opening night and highly recommend the show.  #fortmyers #naples #jwmarriott #serhant
Fort Myers_FL.script: Fort Myers is literally transforming right now and most people are sleeping on it. A 21 story high rise just went vertical downtown for the first time in 20 years. The mayor is calling it a milestone. Developers are betting BIG on this city and honestly they're right. When cranes go up property values follow. We saw this playbook in Tampa. We saw it in St Pete. Now it's Fort Myers turn. The people buying here TODAY are the ones who will be telling their friends "I told you so" in 3 years. Downtown is getting a JW Marriott in Naples. A luxury motorplex is opening between both cities. The infrastructure is catching up to the vision. This isn't speculation anymore. This is happening. I work with buyers every single day in Southwest Florida and the window for pre-boom pricing is closing fast. So real talk... are you still waiting for the "perfect time" to buy here or are you ready to actually move?

Naples_FL.title: This is the part of Naples real estate most people don’t think ...
Naples_FL.caption:
This is the part of Naples real estate most people don’t think about. Calm water, quiet backyards, clean waterfront views, everything designed to feel controlled and predictable, yet across Southwest Florida those same canals are part of a much larger living system that existed long before the homes around them. Waterfront properties, new construction homes, and luxury listings throughout Naples and Collier County are often built directly alongside freshwater canals, lakes, and preserve land, which means wildlife and daily life naturally overlap. American alligators move through thousands of residential waterways across SWFL, often staying completely unnoticed along edges, shadows, and shorelines, requiring very little space and making very little noise. In Naples, development didn’t replace the environment, it formed around it, creating one of the most unique real estate markets in Florida where luxury living and native wildlife exist side by side. It’s a reminder that in Southwest Florida, the lifestyle, the landscape, and the ecosystem are all connected, usually hidden, occasionally visible, and always present. 

#naplesflorida #realestate #swflorida #florida #floridawildlife
Naples_FL.script:
Nobody warns you about this part of Naples living before you buy.

You get the heated pool. You get the brand new pavers. You get that gorgeous pond view every single morning with your coffee.

And then you get the gator checking out your backyard like he pays rent.

Here is the thing though. Locals do not even flinch. Because when you actually live here you realize this is just part of the magic. The wildlife. The water. The feeling that you are somehow living inside a nature documentary but with a really nice kitchen.

Naples buyers who do their homework fall in love with this lifestyle FAST. The ones who skip that step get surprised.

I help people get the full picture before they commit so there are zero surprises after closing.

Are you the type who would think the gator is cool or would that be a dealbreaker for you?

Florida.caption: The Worst Housing Market in America in 2026 is in Florida. Values are already down 15%, they crashed 57% in the last downturn, and foreclosures are spiking. It's Cape Coral-Fort Myers. The Wall Street Journal ran an article on it last year calling it the "Worst Housing Market in the U.S." - now Bloomberg is saying that foreclosures are spiking as rookie home flippers go into default on their mortgages. Last time around, Cape Coral was the "canary in the coal mine" for the rest of the U.S. Will it be again this time around? Find the 12-month price forecast for your area at www.reventure.app.
Florida.author: reventuredata
Florida.title: Reventure Data "The Worst Housing Market in ...
Florida.script:
Cape Coral is flashing the same warning signs we saw right before 2008 and most people are completely ignoring it.

Values are already down 15% from peak. Last crash they fell 57%. That is not a typo.

The Wall Street Journal called it the worst housing market in America. Bloomberg is now reporting foreclosures are surging and abandoned flip projects are showing up everywhere. Half finished homes just sitting there.

Here is what nobody is talking about though. Cape Coral always moves first. It peaked early in 2006 while everyone else was still partying. Then it crashed hard while the rest of Florida was still in denial.

That is exactly what is happening right now.

This market is telling us something. The question is whether you are positioned to protect yourself or take advantage of it.

Are you currently holding property in Southwest Florida or thinking about buying there right now?

Florida.caption: Florida's housing market is crashing in 2026. Sellers are losing money. Prices are down as much as $30,000, $40,000 and even $100,000 in certain neighborhoods. This house is down almost 10% from its 2022 price, and one has to wonder how much more the values could decline. Florida is a boom-or-bust housing market, and there's big long-term growth. But right not there's a bust occurring. Anyone buying in Florida in 2026 needs to understand the 12-month price forecast and overvaluation rate for their area. Access by downloading the Reventure Mobile App or head to www.reventure.app.
Florida.author: reventuredata
Florida.title: Florida's housing market is crashing in 2026. Sellers are ...
Florida.script:
Cape Coral sellers are bleeding right now and most buyers have no idea this is happening.

I just pulled the data and homes that sold in 2022 are now listing BELOW what they paid. One seller bought at 390k and just listed at 357k. That's a 33 thousand dollar loss before closing costs even touch it.

And rentals? Even worse. Landlords are cutting prices and still sitting empty because inventory is through the roof in certain zip codes.

Here's the thing though. This is actually incredible news if you know where to look. Some Cape Coral neighborhoods are holding strong. Some are actually undervalued right now. The people winning in this market are the ones who know the difference before they sign anything.

I work these zip codes every single day and I know exactly which streets are worth your money right now.

So tell me, are you trying to buy or sell in Cape Coral in the next 6 months?

Florida.author: theorlandoreal
Florida.title: Are falling home prices a sign of a crash? 🧐 ㅤ Single family ...
Florida.script:
Florida home prices are dropping in 27 out of 28 markets and everyone's freaking out calling it a crash 👀

But here's what the fear mongers aren't telling you...

Orlando is still up 45% since 2020. That's not a crash. That's a correction after years of insane growth.

Meanwhile yeah Cape Coral is down 17%. Punta Gorda down 22%. Those markets ran WAY too hot too fast.

Not all Florida real estate is the same and that's literally the whole point people are missing right now.

The same people screaming crash in 2020 are still screaming it today. And guess what they got rich selling that fear while smart buyers built serious wealth.

Inflation isn't dropping 30% overnight. Prices have a floor.

So the real question for you is this... are you waiting for a crash that may never come while other buyers are locking in deals RIGHT now in your market?

Florida.caption:
Everyone keeps saying the Florida real estate market is slowing down… but that’s not what I’m seeing on the ground.
Buyers are getting more confident.
Contracts are picking up.
And homes are going under contract faster than people expected.
Here’s what’s really happening: – Interest rates stabilized (that alone calms people down)
– Inventory is still tight in the right price ranges
– And buyers who sat on the sidelines? They’re jumping back in
I’ve personally seen it the last 30 days — especially with pool homes and well-priced properties. The good ones aren’t sitting.
And let’s be real… people are still moving here from high-tax states. That hasn’t changed.
So no… the market isn’t crashing.
It’s shifting — and smart buyers are adjusting.
If you’ve been watching from the sidelines, this is the moment to pay attention.
Comment “Info” for more.
#RockAndRollRealtor #wheretomoveinflorida
Florida.author: rocknrolrealestate
Florida.title: Everyone keeps saying the Florida real estate market is ...
Florida.script:
Everyone keeps saying the Florida real estate market is slowing down. But here is what is actually happening right now.

Consumer confidence in Florida just went up three months straight. And when people feel good about money, they stop waiting and start moving. Literally.

Inflation is cooling. Jobs are stable. And rate cuts are being talked about for later this year. So the buyers who were sitting on the sidelines for two years, they are waking up.

Here in Northeast Florida I am seeing it firsthand. People are flying in, some for the very first time, and on day one they are falling in love and writing offers.

This window we are in right now, it will not stay open forever. The people moving fast are the ones winning the best deals before the crowd catches on.

So tell me, what is the one thing still holding you back from making the move to Florida?

USA.title: A man in Texas struggled for months to sell his house through ...
USA.script:
This Texas homeowner couldn't sell his house for months. Price drops weren't working. Buyers weren't biting. So he stopped playing by the rules.

He turned his listing into a ten dollar contest. People paid ten bucks to enter for a chance to win the house. It went viral overnight. Nearly two hundred thousand people entered.

He walked away with two million dollars. The winner got a house. Everyone else got a story.

Here's what that tells us about today's market. Buyers aren't just looking for a home anymore. They're looking for a reason to care. Generic listings get ignored. Emotion and creativity get attention. Attention gets offers.

The market isn't broken. Your strategy might just need a refresh.

I help sellers in this area think outside the box to attract serious buyers faster and for more money.

What's one thing you wish your last agent had done differently when selling your home?

USA.caption:
2026 is shaping up to be a pivotal year in real estate. 🏡

@therealjasonabrams is watching three pressure points:

1️⃣ Interest rates: They jumped from 3% to nearly 8%. If they keep trending down, demand shifts.

2️⃣ First-time buyers: The average age is 40. If affordability improves, does the next wave step in?

3️⃣ Mortgage lock-ins: Millions sit on 3% rates. If rates ease, does inventory unlock?

What are you watching in 2026? Comment below.
USA.author: kellerwilliamsrealty
USA.title: 2026 is shaping up to be a pivotal year in real estate ...
USA.script:
2026 is about to shake up real estate in a BIG way and here are the 3 things you need to know right now.

Rates. They hit almost 8% back in 2023 and have been slowly dropping. More cuts could be coming this year. That matters for your wallet.

First time buyers. The average age just hit 40. FORTY. As the market softens that number should start dropping again. Meaning more competition for starter homes.

And the big one. Mortgage lock-in. Millions of homeowners are sitting on 3% rates feeling completely stuck. But life happens. New jobs. Babies. Divorces. And when rates drop enough those homes flood the market.

Here is what that means for YOU. More inventory. More opportunity. Possibly your best window in years to buy or sell.

So tell me honestly, are you waiting for rates to drop before you make your move?

USA.caption:
The 2026 housing market is already starting to feel different, especially in Long Beach and Orange County. Buyer motivation is returning in a meaningful way, and many people who have been waiting for the right moment are finally ready to make a move. This shift is happening alongside gradually improving interest rates and new leadership expected at the Fed this May, which has many watching closely for what comes next.

At the same time, more homes are expected to hit the market. Increased inventory means more choices, healthier negotiations, and a more balanced real estate environment than what we have seen over the past several years. Whether you are buying or selling, understanding these changes early can give you a real advantage.

If you are planning a move in 2026, now is the time to get informed and prepared. This is not the same market we have been navigating since 2020.

What are your predictions for the housing market this year? Drop a comment below.

USA.script:
Here's the truth nobody's talking about right now.

Everyone's obsessed with interest rates but that's actually NOT the biggest story heading into 2026.

What I'm seeing on the ground in Long Beach and Orange County is way more interesting.

Motivated buyers are coming back. Like actually motivated. That hasn't happened in years.

Rates are slowly coming down. New Fed leadership hits in May and insiders are already whispering about more cuts coming.

AND inventory is finally opening up. Real options. Real choices. The kind of market where you can actually negotiate again.

This is that window. The one people look back on and say "I wish I moved on that."

The buyers and sellers who act in the next 90 days are going to look like geniuses by end of year.

So tell me something... are you waiting for perfect or are you ready to actually make a move this year?

USA.title: Is 2026 a good year for real estate? According to WFG ...
USA.script:
Here is the script:

2026 is going to be a GOOD year for real estate. But not a great one. And honestly that is actually great news for buyers and sellers who move smart right now.

Here is what the experts at WFG are saying. The economy grows 2 to 3% this year. Capital is flowing. International money is still coming in despite all the tariff noise.

BUT rates are not dropping like people hoped. Why? A 38 trillion dollar national debt with a trillion a year just in interest payments. That is the real story nobody is talking about.

So what does this mean for YOU? The window to get ahead of the crowd is open RIGHT NOW before the spring rush hits and competition goes crazy.

The people waiting for perfect conditions are going to miss the best opportunities of 2026.

Are you trying to buy, sell, or just figure out what your home is worth right now?
